name: Automated Scorer Validation Use Search Pass

search_queries.query: "A Framework for Live Scoring Constructed Response Items" "synthetic data"

inclusion_criteria: The source discusses synthetic, simulated, generated, or altered student or constructed-response text.
The source connects those responses to automated scoring, LLM scoring, scorer calibration, scorer validation, benchmarking, fairness testing, or scoring-data augmentation.
The source provides enough public metadata and study description to stage a source-backed extraction record.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude chatbot or tutoring studies that generate feedback but do not involve response scoring.
Exclude synthetic educational-data studies that do not concern text responses or constructed-response scoring.
Exclude sources that only mention synthetic data generically without a recoverable scorer-use or evaluation boundary.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, and canonical source URL; retained the most stable public source page where multiple mirrors appeared.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 25 web results across targeted queries

screening_decisions.title: A Framework for Live Scoring Constructed Response Items with Commercial LLMs
screening_decisions.reason: Explicitly frames synthetic response datasets as pre-pilot validation data and challenge sets for commercial LLM scoring workflows.

screening_decisions.title: Best of both worlds: combining LLMs and traditional ML for automated scoring of an open-response situational judgment test
screening_decisions.reason: Uses analogous synthetic responses with injected demographic information to pilot fairness analysis of LLM evaluation behavior.

screening_decisions.title: DREsS: Dataset for Rubric-based Essay Scoring on EFL Writing
screening_decisions.reason: Large synthetic essay component is used for training, while real data is reserved for validation and testing; this provides an important boundary for scorer-validation use.

screening_decisions.title: Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Evaluates GPT-generated simulated essays with human scores and realism judgments before using them as automated-scoring augmentation data.

screening_decisions.title: Exploring Data Augmentation Strategies to Enhance AI Scoring of Scientific Explanations
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to synthetic-response augmentation for science explanations, but not needed for the first compact scorer-validation baseline.

screening_decisions.title: Improving Automated Evaluation of Student Text Responses Using GPT-3.5 for Text Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Adjacent augmentation study; deferred to a later generation-methods or augmentation-focused pass.

screening_summary: The first pass found direct support for synthetic responses as pre-pilot validation or challenge data and as a fairness stress-test input, plus adjacent automated-scoring augmentation studies that keep scorer accuracy validation anchored to real or human-scored responses.
limitations: This was a targeted baseline-review search, not a systematic review.
Searches emphasized public web-accessible sources and may miss paywalled assessment conference papers or technical reports.
The initial baseline intentionally stages a small set of high-relevance sources rather than every adjacent augmentation paper.
